flores_passage: Technology determinism is a term that in practice includes a wide range of ideas: for example, the so-called technology push or technology imperative, or the belief that the human destiny is ruled by an all-pervasive logic along with scientific laws and their manifestations in technology. Most interpretations of technological determinism share two general ideas: that the development of technology follows a path that goes far beyond the cultural or political influence, and that technology has on the other hand "influence" on societies that are inherent, rather than societies socially conditioned. We can say, for example, that when motor vehicles appear, it necessarily leads to the development of roads. The national road network is not economically viable for just a few cars, so new production methods are being developed to reduce the cost of owning a car. Mass car ownership also leads to an increase in road accidents, which causes the invention of new techniques in the field of health care, as well as the repair of damaged bodies.

The French electoral law contains a very strict codification of the voting process. Since 1988, the ballot boxes must be transparent so that voters and observers can verify that no other envelopes were placed in the ballot box before the voting began and that no other envelopes were added than those that were placed by the counted and authorized voters.
